fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

telepractice_text: Utah permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Utah issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Utah is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: $100 initial. $52 biennial renewal — one of the lowest renewal fees in the country.

faqs.question: Is Utah part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Utah allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Utah have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Utah participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Utah without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Utah is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
